explanation: Starts all services defined in the docker-compose.yml file. Use the --build flag to recreate images if you changed your code.
Stops and removes all containers, networks, and images defined in the compose file. Adding -v will also remove named volumes.
Lists all currently running containers. Adding -a shows all containers, including those that have stopped.
Runs a container with a persistent volume. This ensures data (like MariaDB databases) is saved on your host machine even if the container is stopped or removed.
Opens an interactive terminal inside a running container so you can inspect files or run commands manually.
Displays the console output (logs) of a specific container. Use -f to follow the logs in real-time.
Removes unused images to free up disk space on your computer.
Gracefully stops a running container without removing it.
Removes a stopped container from your system storage.
A powerful command that removes all stopped containers, unused networks, and dangling images at once.
